title: Predictive History Begins As A School
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Civilization BONUS
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the source. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. This is a personal direct-address source, not a classroom lecture.
dek: Jiang explains the channel from the inside: a teacher leaving Beijing, watching Iran and Israel move toward world war, and trying to turn a student review archive into a new history that can explain the present and predict the future.
thesis: The source is an origin story for predictive history. Jiang's method does not begin as abstract theory. It begins with Chinese students who can read great books but lack historical context, a teacher trying to make all of human history cohere, and a public channel that suddenly becomes a global classroom. The ambition is enormous: true history should connect the past, explain the present, predict the future, and eventually build the foundations for psychohistory. But the source also keeps the project human. Jiang is worried about blood pressure, children, comments he cannot answer, weak points in philosophy and economics, and whether the next version of the course can make the hidden structures of history more visible.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The source begins at a threshold. Jiang is leaving Beijing for Toronto with his sons, checking his health, and then seeing the news that Israel is attacking Iran. The world-war line he had forecast now looks too fast even for him. That is the pressure behind the self-introduction: predictive history is not a brand name. It is a project born from the need to make history usable when events are accelerating. The method has three tests: connect the past into a coherent story, explain the present, and predict the future.

marks.text: World War III far faster and far harder than I could ever imagine
connect events of the past into a coherent story

beats.heading: The Threshold Morning
beats.summary: Family travel, health anxiety, and geopolitical escalation arrive in the same morning.

paragraphs.text: This is not a normal course installment. It is the last day in Beijing before Jiang flies to Toronto with his boys. His wife is worried about his blood pressure. His children are about to see their grandparents. Then the news arrives: Israel is attacking Iran. The domestic and the historical are not separate scenes. They meet in one morning.

The political point is sharp because it is dated. Jiang says prior videos anticipated this direction, but not this accelerated timeline. The claim is not simply that World War III may happen. It is that the timeline is outrunning even the person trying to predict it.

beats.heading: The Teacher Before The Theory
beats.summary: The intellectual project begins from education: immigrant biography, Yale literature, Chinese classrooms, and students without historical context.

paragraphs.text: The biography is told as an education arc. Born in China, raised in Toronto, poor family, Yale scholarship, English literature, return to China: each step leads back to teaching. Jiang describes himself less as a pundit than as someone who has worked across levels of Chinese education trying to make reform concrete.

The first great-books experiment works because the books have force: Julius Caesar, Paradise Lost, Virginia Woolf, Dante, the Iliad, the Bible. But success reveals the problem. Chinese students can love the texts and still lack the historical world that makes them intelligible. The history course grows out of that missing context.

marks.text: absolutely no historical context to work with

beats.heading: The Test Of True History
beats.summary: Predictive history is named as a new movement and tied directly to Asimov's psychohistory.

paragraphs.text: The crucial definition arrives after the course story. Predictive history is history that does three things at once: it connects the past into a coherent story, explains the present, and predicts the future. Anything less is incomplete. True history should help humanity organize, think, and control its future.

marks.text: then it must be true history

paragraphs.text: Asimov is not a decorative influence. The Foundation series gives Jiang the image of psychohistory: modeling past and present so the future becomes predictable. The long-term ambition is to build intellectual foundations for that possibility, not merely to comment on history class by class.

marks.text: build intellectual foundations for the possibility of psychohistory

beats.heading: The Archive Becomes A Classroom
beats.summary: A student review archive suddenly becomes a public channel with a global audience.

paragraphs.text: The channel begins modestly: uploaded lectures so Chinese students with imperfect English can review class. Then the scale changes. A few hundred subscribers become roughly 25,000 in about a month. A tool built for one classroom becomes a global classroom before Jiang has time to build the infrastructure for it.

The gratitude is real, but so are the limits. Jiang cannot answer every comment while caring for three children, prepping classes, getting sick, and living through Beijing's allergy season and COVID. This matters because the public role is not polished yet. The teacher is still visibly becoming the public intellectual.

beats.heading: The Summer Program
beats.summary: The next course plan, the geopolitics turn, and the dream of a school make the channel into an institution in embryo.

paragraphs.text: The summer is not a pause. It is revision. Jiang says he needs to read, reflect, and repair weak points in philosophy and economics. The admission matters: the project is predictive, but it is not presented as finished. It has to learn in public.

marks.text: the weak point in my analysis

paragraphs.text: The next version of the course should compress 60 classes into 30, add new material, and make the underlying structures of human history clearer. After that comes geopolitics: current events, causal explanation, and prediction. The method moves from the whole arc of human history into live events.

marks.text: the underlying structures of human history
making predictions about what will happen

paragraphs.text: The final ambition is institutional. Jiang wants a school for liberal arts and humanities that builds psychohistory, something like Plato's Academy and something like a Jedi Temple. The phrase is playful, but the dream is serious: train future intellectuals, writers, and historians who can help lead humanity forward.

source_notes.text: This is a short direct-address bonus video published on 2025-06-13 after the first 60-class history course, not a normal classroom lecture.

archive_note: No source questions are captured in the transcript; references to comments are Jiang summarizing viewer feedback.
